113:                         state_d = Write;

115: 			state_d = Read; //reads input password
116: 			if (data_d == pass) begin //checks password
117: 			    pass_chk = 1'b1;	
118: 			end
119: 			state_d = Idle;
120: 		    end
121:                     // else this is a nop and we can stay here
122:                 end
123:             end
124: 
125:             Read: begin
126:                 dmi_req_valid = 1'b1;
127:                 if (dmi_req_ready) begin
128:                     state_d = WaitReadValid;
129:                 end
130:             end
131: 
132:             WaitReadValid: begin
133:                 // load data into register and shift out
134:                 if (dmi_resp_valid) begin
135:                     data_d = dmi_resp.data;
136:                     state_d = Idle;
137:                 end
138:             end
139: 
140:             Write: begin
141:                 dmi_req_valid = 1'b1;
142:                 // got a valid answer go back to idle
143:                 if (dmi_req_ready) begin
144:                     state_d = Idle;
145:                 end
146:             end
147: 
148:             WaitWriteValid: begin
149:                 // just wait for idle here
150:                 if (dmi_resp_valid) begin
151:                     state_d = Idle;
152:                 end
153:             end

5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule
